behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at parser/aggregator handoff when real git rename records are interpreted. REAL-INFRA: this behavior depends on real git CLI/subprocess rename detection and output format details. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked status payload can be shaped to pass while real git output still breaks categorization. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with a stubbed `R100 old -> new` record passes, but the real subprocess returns a different rename form (or D+A when rename detection flags are wrong), causing double-counting.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, then run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo. Assert stdout report has the file only in `renamed`, with no corresponding increment in `added` or `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk appears when real binary diff output is consumed across modules. REAL-INFRA: requires real git diff behavior on binary blobs via subprocess/filesystem. MOCK-HIDE: stubs avoid real encoding/format edge cases and can hide decode/parsing crashes. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test feeding sanitized fake diff metadata passes, while real git output for binary files triggers a Unicode decode/parsing exception.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, commit a binary file change (or add/delete binary files), invoke the CLI subprocess, and assert exit code is success and binary files are reflected in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is interaction with git repo state before first commit. REAL-INFRA: real git commands on unborn HEAD return specific non-zero exit/status behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repo adapters typically return empty data and miss real error codes/stderr handling. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Integration with a fake git layer returning `[]` commits passes, but real `git` subprocess returns exit 128 for HEAD-dependent calls and the CLI errors.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and an empty structured report (all counts zero/no entries), not an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on end-to-end interpretation of real history across git query/parsing/aggregation modules. REAL-INFRA: requires actual repo history and likely git subprocess semantics. MOCK-HIDE: fabricated history fixtures can miss merge/rename/mode-change nuances that occur in real repos. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Integration tests with curated fake commit events pass, but on a real repo containing rename+modify sequences the CLI miscounts categories.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp real repo with a known sequence of commits covering add/delete/rename/modify, run the CLI subprocess, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from git commands for that repo.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when comparing two real git states (index vs working tree) through full CLI path. REAL-INFRA: requires real git index/worktree behavior and subprocess interactions. MOCK-HIDE: stubs often return equivalent datasets and miss true staged/unstaged diff command differences. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Integration test with mocked diff provider returning the same changes for both modes passes, but real `git diff` vs `git diff --cached` paths produce divergent parsing/output.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, invoke CLI in both contexts, and assert the structured stdout payloads are byte-for-byte identical (or structurally identical after canonical serialization).
